one_time_description: Full allowance recorded against investment in Cross Lake Mineral Exploration Inc. (loans/advances of $2,627,211 written down to nil)
economic_notes: Midnorth Development Corporation provides automotive, repair and construction services. Cross Lake Mineral Exploration Inc. is developing the Pipestone Vanadium Project. Tree Suns Junction Inc. operates Ponton Service Station (1997) Ltd. and My's Est. 98 Ltd. (gas station and restaurant off-reserve, no operations during year). Cross Lake Place operates a gas station. New hospital facility under construction funded by ISC.
notes: Fiscal year is March 31, 2022 (not 2024). Province is Manitoba. Consolidated entity includes Midnorth Development Corporation, Cross Lake Multi-Channel Systems, Tree Suns Junction Inc. (Ponton Service Station and My's Est. 98 Ltd.), and Cross Lake Place as fully consolidated subsidiaries. Cross Lake Mineral Exploration Inc. is accounted for under modified equity method (GBE) and had full allowance recorded in 2022 against $2,627,211 in loans/advances. Large increase in TCA ($127M vs $80M prior year) due to $52.8M in capital purchases including new hospital project. Capital lease obligations of $3,419,963 exist in addition to long-term debt. Due from Colliers of $5,463,663 included in accounts receivable. Northern Flood Agreement receivable of $524,108. ISC hospital project restricted cash of $7,186,980. total_government_transfers calculated as ISC $135,161,584 + CMHC $2,049,117 + HRSDC $2,061,700 + Province of Manitoba $965,494 = $140,237,895. osr calculated as total revenue $156,201,725 minus government transfers $140,237,895 = $15,963,830 (includes Midnorth revenues, other revenue, investment income, rental income, deferred revenue movements). annual_debt_service reflects long-term debt repayments only; capital lease repayments were an additional $1,432,954.
warnings: One-time items ($1) represent 0.0% of revenue — surplus may not reflect ongoing operations
High ISC dependency (86.53%) — operations heavily reliant on federal transfers
